CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-15-2023 Off Calendar

Plaintiff???S Summary Of Case In Support Of Request For Entry Of Default Judgment By Court (Notices)
Off Calendar Jun-02-2023 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for request for entry of default judgment by Court.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Shanika Monroe; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 02:05:35 p.m. and concluding at 02:37:35 p.m.

Plaintiff American Express National Bank represented by Jennifer L. Shelfer,Esq. via Zoom Video Conference, William Huckinsm, Esq., and Mikayla O'Neal, Esq.
Defendant Empire Engineering & Construction, Inc. a California Corporation not appearing.

DAVID MILKULECKY (In House Counsel for American Express Bank) is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

Court determines Defendant was served with Statement of Damages and confers with Plantiff's counsel to finalize proposed judgment.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents case summary and an offer of proof.
Court examines DAVID MILKULECKY.

The following exhibits are MARKED/ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE:

Plaintiff's Exhibit A - Application
Plaintiff's Exhibit B - Demand Letter
Plaintiff's Exhibit C - Contract

Court examines Counsel for Plaintiff regarding attorney fees and its preparation of default packet. Court further examines DAVID MILKULECKY. Witness attests that statements made by its counsel are true and correct.

The matter is submitted.
COURT FINDS THE FOLLOWING:

1. The Complaint filed Mar-21-2022 is the operative pleading
2. If judgment is entered as requested, it will resolve the entire action
3. Defendant was served with Complaint Mar-31-2022. Proof of Service filed Jun-09-2022
4. Statement of Damages was served May-09-2023 on Defendant and proof of service filed May-10-2023
5. There are no DOE Defendants
6. Default entered as to Defendant Empire Engineering & Construction, Inc. a California Corporation Jun-13-2022

DECLARATION OF DAVID MIKULECKY IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF'S APPLICATION FOR CLERK'S JUDGMENT FILED DEC-14-2022 & SUPPLEMENTAL DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF'S APPLICATION FOR COURT JUDGMENT FILED MAY-12-2023 AND EXHIBITS ATTACHED ARE ADMITTED INTO EVIDENCE

Based on the summary of the case, comments made by Plaintiff's Counsel, Plaintiff's witness, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has proven each essential element of the complaint and Defendant is in breach of loan agreement and it failed to pay balance of loan. The principle amount has not been paid.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff American Express National Bank, and against Empire Engineering & Construction, Inc. a California Corporation, as follows:

1. Damages: $159,065.08
2. Prejudgment Interest: $1,792.78
3. Attorney Fees: $3,080.00
4. Costs: $52.05

TOTAL JUDGMENT $163,989.91
Proposed judgment signed, filed, and served in open court.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-14-2023 Continued To Jul-12-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff American Express National Bank appearing represented by Mikayla O'Neal Esq.
Defendant Empire Engineering & Construction Inc. not appearing.

DAVID MIKLUECKY is sworn to testify on behalf of Plaintiff.

Court addresses Counsel for Plaintiff re: Statment of Damages and Proposed Judgment.

Court orders matter continued to June 2, 2023 at 1:30 p.m. in Department 505 to allow Plaintiff's Counsel to file and serve a Statment of Damages on Defendant and submit a proposed judgment to the Court. (D505)
